page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1571.09 and a multi-core score of 33012.14. However, it demonstrates strong memory write capabilities, achieving 93.48 GB/sec in large-block uncached write benchmarks, which ranks in the top 25 percent. Read bandwidth remains average at 1856.97 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 165.51 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform at average levels of 26.77 GB/sec and 125.32 GB/sec, respectively.

The absence of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring users to provision external block storage for data persistence. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe drives but benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density. The instance is well-suited for enterprise databases, in-memory caching systems, data analytics, and memory-intensive business applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB vCPUs running at 3.3 GHz and 256.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average CPU performance with a stress-ng multi-core score of 33012.14, but highlights strong memory write performance at 93.48 GB/sec. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that utilize network-attached storage. It is suitable for databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding database and caching workloads.
